title: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: Independent Two-Stage Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: Long-term growth aligning with GDP growth, as construction and mining machinery will always be fundamentally tied to global economic expansion.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

items.title: Cyclicality
items.description: Heavy reliance on the construction and mining sectors, which are highly sensitive to economic cycles and interest rates.

items.title: Currency Exposure
items.description: As a global exporter, strong USD negatively impacts foreign earnings conversion.

items.title: Supply Chain & Commodities
items.description: Steel and component costs can pressure margins if pricing power doesn't keep up.

items.title: Geopolitics
items.description: Trade tensions or tariffs can significantly affect international sales.

faq.question: Why use a 6% growth rate for Caterpillar?
faq.answer: While historically cyclical, CAT's increasing shift toward autonomous machinery, recurring services, and parts revenue helps smooth out cash flows. A 6% rate balances its maturity with its strong pricing power and global infrastructure growth tailwinds.

faq.question: Why is the discount rate set to 8.5%?
faq.answer: 8.5% reflects CAT's beta (around 1.1), the current 10-year treasury yield of 4.18%, and a reasonable equity risk premium. It accounts for the inherent cyclical risks of the heavy machinery sector while recognizing the company's strong balance sheet.

faq.question: Is Caterpillar currently overvalued or undervalued?
faq.answer: Based on my DCF analysis, the calculated intrinsic value is $243.62. Compared to the current price of $707.06, it appears to be overvalued.
